entry.title: Re: ST12 Trace - Step by step instruction on how to use it for analysis
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have a small query here. User has ran the trace 3 times and each trace it has different number of traces collected. Why does this difference is. 

 

 

Regards,

P Ramakrishna

entry.title: Re: Credit Check Rule
entry.content:
Dear Johan,

 

The Check Rules 01, 02 and 03 as defined in the configuration step Define Checking Rules (SSCUI 102623) are SAP standard entries and should not be changed. You can either use these pre-delivered check rules or create your own check rules in customer namespace either Z* or Y* and set them as your default if needed.

Further information regarding the function of Check Rules and the available Check Steps can be found in the SAP Help Portal Credit Check 

 

 

Best Regards,

Priyanka

entry.title: Re: Change default delivery address of plant when creatin...
entry.content:
Hi 

I think Your issue can be resolved by using MEAN transaction. It is used to maintain delivery address and then you can find it in delivery tab at item level in PO and you can pull it from there see the screen shot address field. Hope it will work

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: 'XS advance login page' error

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

I’m Samia, a recent graduate in economics, passionate about using data to drive business decisions. I’ve developed a keen interest in SAP, particularly in its planning and financial management tools. I’m working toward getting certified through the SAP Digital Skills Initiative to strengthen my expertise in these areas. I’m excited to leverage SAP’s solutions to make meaningful contributions in strategic planning and functional roles in the future.

Looking forward to growing in this field!

entry.title: Re: Leave Carry Forward via Interim Rule
entry.content:
hello Poonam,

can we set rules to avoid coworkers to take more than 50% of current leave entitlement for their upcoming leave application in a new year

entry.title: Re: Why WOCR is not getting selected in WO instead only D...
entry.content: "No activity area was transferred"; Did you sort the Bins? Do they have the right activity area? Please check in /SCWM/LS03 and provide a screenshot.

entry.title: Re: Why WOCR is not getting selected in WO instead only D...
entry.content: The marked line says "The search sequence for activity area is used". There is just space between "area" and "is". Two lines above it says "No activity area was transferred", that is even yellow, that is a warning. So no activity area was used for the determination. Do your bins show the activity area you want for the correct activity? I guess not.

entry.title: Celebrating (almost) 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Celebrating (almost) 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative

In less than two years, the Digital Skills Initiative has made a real impact on people’s lives, helping them gain certification and secure employment within the SAP ecosystem.

October 2024 marks 22 months since the Digital Skills Initiative first launched. Our mission from the outset has been simple: employ new people from underrepresented groups in the SAP ecosystem. Today, we’re proud to celebrate the success and hard work of all participants and partners involved.

The initiative has provided two main avenues for success: a self-study track and instructor-led boot camps. We’ve seen people from all walks of life, including those who may not have had the opportunity otherwise, excel in these programs and go on to achieve certification. Hundreds of participants have not only earned their certifications, but many have also secured jobs (or promotions) with SAP partners or SAP itself, launching promising careers.

Self-Study Success

One of the cornerstones of the initiative has been the self-study track. This flexible option has allowed participants to learn at their own pace, fitting their studies around existing commitments. Many applied and a determined and disciplined few achieved certification and career advancement as a result.

One shining example of a self-study success story is Lin Fan in Germany:

Breaking Barriers and Fighting Sexism: Lin’s Journey from a Salesgirl to an SAP Consultant

Lin has been incredibly supportive of the program following her success and has since started working at SAP. Her decision to start networking and self-studying toward her first certification served as a clear display of motivation for hiring managers.

Boot Camp Triumphs

Our instructor-led boot camps have been intensive, hands-on experiences designed to fast-track participants towards certification. These events provided not only technical training but also mentoring and networking opportunities, bridging the gap between education and employment.

Of course one of our shining bootcamp stars is Chelcie Scott, whose tenacity and persistence were unmatched – leading her to achieve SAP Certification and a start her career in the SAP Ecosystem with one of our partners:

Breaking Down Barriers: Hear from Chelcie Scott How She Broke into Tech with the Help of SAP

Changing Lives, One Certification at a Time

For some, earning an SAP certification has been a life-changing milestone, opening doors to new career opportunities, promotions and stable employment. The feedback from our participants has been overwhelmingly positive and while most of our success stories shall remain anonymous, I’d like to share a few more to drive the point home:

 Learning Her Way Up: The Inspiring Growth Story of Anu in Norway

Beating the Odds: How SAP Skills Shaped Luca’s Career Despite a Struggling Economy

Empowering Dreams Through Digital Skills: A Glimpse Into Karla's Tech Journey with SAP

Areeba's Journey to Learn SAP Skills: Shattering Glass Ceilings in Pakistan

Ashvini's Journey: Reimagining Success through SAP in a New Country

Shoutout to @Anne_Lenhardt  for communicating these stories for us!                                                                                                                                                                                          

The Digital Skills Initiative would not have been possible without the support of our dedicated instructors, SAP Partners, and most importantly, the determination of our participants. Together, we've created a community that supports learning and growth, fostering a more inclusive future within the SAP ecosystem.

As we approach our two-year anniversary, we eagerly look forward to seeing more individuals succeed and transform their lives through this initiative.

For anyone interested in the self-study route of this initiative, please write to us here. Time is of the essence as we will be closing out the self-study program by 4 November 2024. (Once you get your free certification exam attempt you will have 12 months to study for and take the exam.)

Stay tuned for what’s next!

-David

entry.title: Where can I start learning Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Solution (BRIM)?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering the capabilities of SAP Solutions for Quote-to-Cash Management private cloud (BRIM) for Business User learning journey


Overview
The course describes the processes in SAP CPQ and SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM). 
The training gives a process overview about SAP CPQ, SAP Solution Quotation, SAP SOM Subscription Order Management, SAP Convergent Charging, SAP Convergent Mediation, SAP Convergent Invoicing and Financial Contract Accounting. 
Use cases in different industries (High Tech, Postal, Telecommunication) describe how business to customer processes (B2C) and business to business processes (B2B) for subscription and consumption business are implemented with SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM).

Learning objectives

After completing this beginner-level learning journey, Business Users will be able to:

  • Understand the Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Solution (BRIM) components.
  • Understand BRIM processes with help of one business example configurable cloud product.
  • Through system demo recordings, Business Users will learn how the BRIM process runs; starting from Solution quotation, rate usage, run dunning process and end with a credit note.
  • Identify Basic Objects in a BRIM Landscape.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Some standard fields unable to find in standard form template
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Why WOCR is not getting selected in WO instead only D...
entry.content: Bin Sorting was done as suggested by Laurin Paul, issue resolved , thanks Paul once again

entry.title: Co-ordinate maintenance and new development with gCTS
entry.content:
If you develop your own applications in an ABAP system or need to manage customizing, you might soon reach a point where two types of changes are required at the same time: fix something because an issue has been discovered by your business users and new feature ideas that have to be implemented. The challenge is to be able to continue new development while fixing something else without adding the new features to production when you deliver the fix.
There are processes in the ‘good old’ world of ABAP that have evolved over the years and that are widely used to manage this challenge. And for sure you can continue to use them.
But what if you consider using the git-enabled CTS (gCTS)? How do processes change? What are the tools that you can use?
To say that from the beginning: with gCTS, you can handle your own development objects and customizing. For maintenance in the sense of implementing service packs provided by SAP, SAP Notes. Or for managing modifications that you might have done to SAP-owned objects, you need to use CTS when it comes to transports.
And in addition: in most of the cases, you would still need the same number of systems: separate development systems for feature development and maintenance and maybe also separated test systems. This is because the basic paradigm of ABAP that there can only be one active version of an object in a system at a time is not changed with gCTS. gCTS is another way of storing versions and managing what is deployed or imported where but the basics of ABAP are not subject to the competences of gCTS.
So, what can you do?
In principle, gCTS offers the option to work with a single system landscape consisting of a development, a test and a production system. Different versions of objects can be reflected by the help of different branches. The branches of your repository could then for example look like in the following figure:

Production would be the active branch on the production system – that’s easy 😉
For development and test systems, you would need to switch branches. Maybe the standard would be that the development and test system have the feature branch as active branch so that new functionality can be created.
In case an issue comes up in production, development and test need to be switched to a maintenance branch. This branch should be created out of the current state of the production or main branch. This branch stays active until fixes are done. Then the branch with the fix needs to be merged into development, main and production. After all tests are finished and the fix has arrived in production, the branch containing the fix can be deleted and the development branch can again become active in the development and test system.
Looks like a great approach to reduce the number of systems? It is, when you just look at the systems. But you need to consider our workforce… while a fix is done, no new development is possible. So, if you do not develop a lot and only have very few developers, this might be an option. But if more people – and in this case already two to three might be considered as ‘more – this approach comes with challenges and a lot of dele time. You have to carefully coordinate when you do what: switching a branch means that the coding of that branch is to become active. Changes to the same objects that are not released (and therewith committed to the repository) will be overwritten, which means that they are lost after the branch switch. Developers working on new features cannot continue their work while a maintenance branch is active and have to wait until all activities around fixing the big are finished.
So, using this approach to reduce the number of systems requires good planning and organizational measures to make sure that no work gets lost, and it can result in times where development is not possible for some of your team members.
If reducing the number of systems is not your main goal, you can nevertheless profit from using gCTS to co-ordinate maintenance and development within your system landscape. You would need two development systems and most probably two test systems, as well. One development and one test system are used for maintenance and the respective other ones for new development. Only the systems that you currently need have to be up and running. The branch design of the repository is the same as described above. The maintenance systems are connected to the production branch as long as no fix is done and are by default shut down. When a fix is needed, the first step (after having started the systems for sure) would be to create a new branch out of the production branch for the required fix and make this branch the active one in the maintenance development and test system. Only after that, you should open up the system for developers to do the fix. When the fix is successfully tested, the branch needs to get merged into all other branches including the development branch. Now, maintenance systems need to get closed for development. The fix can be deployed to production, the production branch becomes again the active branch in the maintenance systems and the branch that was used for fixing should be deleted. With this, you can make sure that you get the current production state of your software is available in the maintenance systems whenever a fix has to be done.
On the system for new development, you only need a short pause in development when a fix has been merged into the development branch to pull the changes and at some point in the merge process, you also have to solve conflicts in case an object is concerned by the fix that you also changed for new features. gCTS supports you in detecting the conflicts and solving them.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2405 Sync issues
entry.content:
We are very frequently getting -  HTTP Code 401 with message: <html><meta http-equiv="refresh" content="0"><body onload="Javascript:document.location.reload(tru e);"><H1>Your browser is not configured for using SPNego<) during sync. 

But after synchronising for couple of times, this error goes away and the sync is successful.

When we get above error, in the gateway traces we do not see any requests coming in, so we are currently under the impression that this issue must be because of either the web dispatcher or the cloud connector.

We have checked the logs but unable to find any leads on this issue. Any inputs please on this issue?

# SAP Service and Asset Manager

entry.title: Re: BDC Page scroll Issue
entry.content:
Hello @hossain_rajib55 

Why don't you use WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE function or Delivery Interface to update picking quantities and post GI? It's documented, supported, much easier than error prone BDC.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SD/EWM Real Open Quantities in Sales Schedule Agreeme...
Re: SD/EWM Real Open Quantities in Sales Schedule Agreement / Schedule Lines
entry.content:
Hi RAF, Thanks for the quick response, I forgot to mention we are using sales schedule agreement + schedule lines (forecast delivery schedule) not sales orders, here is our configuration right now: 

Av. CheckDescriptionRule Availability CheckAvail. Check Rule
SPStock and Plan. Rec.Sales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)
SRStock and Rel. Rec.Sales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)
STConsider Only StockSales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)
ZPStock & Plan Rec KCSales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)
ZRStock & Rel Rec KCSales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)
ZTConsid Only Stock KCSales OrderDeliverySD Order MTSSD Order ConsDialog Box (Batch/BAPI Delivery Proposal)

Sales Order Items - Backorders looks very good, but unfortunately only shows Sales Orders and cannot be filtered by another SD document category such as Sales Schedule Agreement. Any suggestions?

entry.title: Similar use of FM SAVE_TEXT, READ_TEXT in S4 Hana cloud for Standard Text
entry.content:
We want to use the data given in the standard text app Manage Standard Text in our RAP Class Eclipse ADT.

  1. How to read the data given in the standard text in RAP Class Eclipse ADT?
  2. How to save additional data in the given standard text through RAP Class in Eclipse ADT?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Similar use of FM SAVE_TEXT, READ_TEXT in S4 Hana clo...
entry.content:
1. In S/4HANA CLoud the CDS view I_OperationStandardText for reading Standard Text  ( https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/I_OPERATIONSTANDARDTEXT )

2. Unfortunately there is no API similar to SAVE_TEXT to update Standard operation Text . Please submit a feature request if you would like it added

entry.title: To print the attached PDF files to a maintenance operation in a new SAP Form Template.
entry.content:
Is it possible to print all the PDF attachment for a maintenance operation in the new form which is being developed similar to Operation Control Ticket? What challenges are there to develop this functionality? 

Can someone tell us the steps or the feasibility for this? Which all configuration needs to be maintained to have or use the new form to appear in the work order print function?

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content: I realize the importance of continuous learning and growth in an ever-evolving tech landscape. Pursuing this certification is not just about adding another credential—it's about deepening my expertise, validating the skills I've gained, and positioning myself to better solve complex challenges in my field. By staying current and certified, I can contribute more effectively to my team's success and drive innovation with confidence. This certification aligns with my long-term career goals and the demands of the industry, making it a vital step forward @David_Chaviano

entry.title: Re: BDC Page scroll Issue
entry.content:
@Sandra_Rossi  Please check now. Scroll is working but it skip one line . In internal table i got all the values without interval.

@DominikTylczyn  I create a BAPI for doing it automatically using API.

entry.title: MIR4 - Add custom fields in vendor data subscreen 6510 in program SAPLMR1M
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I'm having the request to add custom fields in program SAPLMR1M subscreen 6510 from MIR4 / MIRO transaction.

I already checked tons of posts but I didn't find a clear solution without adapt standard code.

I found some tutorials to add custom tab in MIRO or add custom fields in the header but nothing concerning this specific screen 6510 that concerns the vendor information.

I already checked BADI's MRM_ITEM_CUSTFIELDS or BADI_FDCB_SUBBAS* and also all the user-exist or MIRO transaction

Exit Name Description

LMR1M001 Exit utilisateur dans le contrôle des factures de logistique
LMR1M002 Regroupement des comptes pour détermination comptes EM/EF
LMR1M003 Attribution numéros ds contrôle des factures de logistique
LMR1M004 Contrôle factures logist. : textes postes pour doc. suivants
LMR1M005 Ctrle factures logist. : validation pr enreg. doc. préenreg.
MRMH0001 Contrôle factures logistique : facturation automatique
MRMH0002 Contrôle des factures logistique : message EDI entrant
MRMH0003 Contrôle des factures logistique : revalorisation/RAP
MRMN0001 Edition/Création de messages - contrôle factures logistique
LMR1M006 Contrôle des factures logistique : traiter facture XML

Can you please tell me if anyone already had this kind of request ?
Thank you for your help !

https://www.sapyard.com/steps-to-add-custom-tab-in-standard-miro-item-screen-using-badi-mrm_item_custfields/

https://sapyard.com/add-custom-fields-in-standard-miro-header-screen-through-badi_fdcb_subbas01/

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Vpe511bIsE8

https://abapers.wordpress.com/tag/olmrlist/

Vivek

For example as shown below:

Thanks and Regards,

Nikitha

entry.title: Re: MIR4 - Add custom fields in vendor data subscreen 6510 in program SAPLMR1M
entry.content: First of all, look at SAPLMR1M subscreen 6510, did you see any place for generic content? It takes 30 seconds. I didn't see one. So, the only solution left is a modification of standard.

entry.title: Re: BDC Page scroll Issue
entry.content: Its done. Thanks for reply

entry.title: Re: Error : com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.OsciException:
entry.content: is it resolved ? if yes then how

entry.title: SAC Excel Add In Override
entry.content:
Hi Folks 

When using SAC excel add in on a planning model we have an initial front sheet which allows the users to select the members for executing the planning template against for cost information

We have two more tabs in excel - one for Headcount and one for FTE

The second two tabs should use the same selected members that were used on the initial sheet 

To achieve this I have used the SAP.OVERWRITEDIMENSIONFILTER on the second two tabs referring back to the initial member selection

When i execute a "Refresh All" I am having to then go into each tab and run the refresh all / functions again (sometime 2 or 3 times) to get the override to kick in . Seems flaky

Recording an excel macro does not work and using the Ribbon bar identifier does not work as everyone has different add in for excel so the reference is not consistent

Question

Has anyone figure out how to make the SAP.OVERWRITEDIMENSIONFILTER be executed on the first refresh and bring back the correct data on Tab 2 and 3 ?

 

Thanks

Russell

entry.title: Re: BDC Page scroll Issue
entry.content:
You are positioning the item number 00020 at the line 1. At the next item number, you are positioning it again at the line 1.

So, you should ALWAYS refer to LIPS-LGORT(1), LIPSD-G_LFIMG(1), LIPSD-PIKMG(1).

Is that clear?

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM integration with MS Teams notifications for Features and Transports
entry.content:
I'm looking at integration with MS Teams for notifications, the standard tasks and health monitoring is simple enough, as there are template mappings to copy.   
I'd like something for notifications around transport approval, either something is ready to test or requires approval to production. 
I can't find any information on this, is it possible and can you point me in the right direction if it is please.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM integration with MS Teams notifications...
entry.content:
Currently SAP Cloud ALM sends notifications to MS Teams by means of the "External API Management" app configurations. 
This allows Customers to create a Subscription, which uses a Mapping to transform an SAP Cloud ALM payload into a format that the target API can process.

SAP Cloud ALM has a Resource Changes API which has a resource for Features.
This allows Customers to route information about Feature updates to an API of their choice (e.g. MS Teams).
You can use the information from the API guide links to help construct a mapping that will allow you to transform Feature payload information into MS Teams chats.
Please note, the API is for Features, not for the Transport Requests within them.
However, you can create a mapping that will send MS Teams notifications for certain Feature status values, achieving something close to the workflow you have descibed.

entry.title: Showcase your security competence with our new SAP security operations certification
entry.content:
As companies transition their SAP landscapes to the cloud, strong security measures are essential to protect sensitive data and ensure system stability. To address this, we are introducing a new certification for partners who support SAP customers in managing security during cloud migration and ongoing cloud operations: SAP security operations certification.

Security is indispensable for companies migrating their SAP landscapes to the cloud. The importance of safeguarding sensitive data and maintaining system integrity cannot be overstated. As businesses migrate to the cloud, they seek guidance from their partners to implement robust security measures. 

Customers expect risk mitigation strategies, including attack surface reduction, identity and access management, and compliance controls. By offering these security measures, partners can ensure that their SAP customers confidently embrace cloud innovation, knowing their systems are resilient and protected. 

We are introducing a new certification for partners who help SAP customers manage their security during cloud migration and management of live SAP cloud solutions and landscapes. While SAP is responsible for the security of many layers of SAP cloud solutions, the certification covers additional areas and boundaries that are the customers’ responsibility.  The SAP security operations certification focuses on partners whose service portfolios include these security operations services for SAP’s cloud customers. 

To be qualified for this SAP security operations certification partners are audited for adherence to SAP’s security guidelines while offering security services for SAP cloud solutions and landscapes like identity & access management, SAP GRC, cybersecurity, attack surface reduction, enterprise threat detection, data privacy & protection, management of SAP application security, along with secure configuration, hyperscaler connectivity, and SAP development, to name a few. 

To ensure the new certification effectively covers the right topics and that auditors ask the most relevant questions, we conducted a pilot phase with two selected partners. The feedback was overwhelmingly positive, helping us fine-tune and enhance the certification offering. Even before launching, SAP partners expressed a clear demand for a certification that highlights their expertise in SAP security operations. With this in mind, we are confident that our new certification service will generate strong interest in the market.  

Partners who are SAP-certified in SAP security operations showcase their expertise, knowledge, and experience across a broad spectrum of security areas. This certification highlights their technical and process competencies, ensuring they can effectively manage and secure SAP cloud solutions for their customers. By choosing certified partners for security operations, customers not only protect themselves against potential cyber-attacks but also build trust with their stakeholders, demonstrating their commitment to maintaining the highest standards of data protection and privacy. 

Information on the certification program for operations partners and an overview of all certified partners can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com

entry.title: Re: SAP Omnichannel Promotion Pricing - Enable outbound processing using SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello Wolfgang Sattler,

I hope you're doing well. I’m currently integrating SAP OPPS with SAP Business One and need some clarification regarding the schema for retrieving Merchandise Set Inclusion/Exclusion products. At the moment, I’m referencing the /MerchandiseSetNodes endpoint, but my goal is to obtain promotionID, MerchandiseSetID, and itemID.

Could you please guide me on the correct schema or approach for this?

Regards,

Pradeep

entry.title: Re: Badi implementation in fb70
entry.content:
Are you saying that your system has the standard method GET_DATA_REFERENCE? I guess you had checked twice before asking me to confirm, so better check three times.

ASSIGN COMPONENT 'XXXX' OF STRUCTURE <ls_data> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<XXXX>).

There are lots of documentation, blog posts and answers about field symbols.

entry.title: Re: MIR4 - Add custom fields in vendor data subscreen 651...
entry.content: With transaction SHD0 we have the possibility to hide some information so I was thinking about hide some and add additional fields...This is why I posted this request. I added links about SHD0 transaction also.

entry.title: Re: Block PO creation if not associated to PR
entry.content:
Hi 

Could you see this note it may be help you. solutions part I copy here

 664424 'Requisition number not mandatory in

If the requisition needs to be entered in ME21N, you will need to customize a Function Authorization and then assign this to all users forwhom this needs to be mandatory, using parameter EFB

This is done in the IMG path:
Material Management
Purchasing
Authorization Management
Define Function Authorizations for Buyers
In here choose: Function Authorizations: Purchase Order

This customizing will only deal with the fields found in box Possiblref objects of a Authorization function.

Untick w/o reference
Tick ref. to PReq. (and maybe the two related fields right below)
Please note, that when you untick w/o reference, then all other fields in the "Possible ref objects" box becomes effective.
Therefore, if you only want the requisition to become mandatory, you should only tick ref. to PReq (and maybe the two related fields right below) and leave all other fields in that box unticked.

Please also note that this customizing will not make the field Requisition mandatory in ME21N!
However, if you try to make a PO without having entered the requisisiton you will get error MEPO 063; "You have no authorization to create without reference to another document".

IMPORTANT:
This workaround only works in the Item Overview in ME21N. If you enter the schedule lines in the Item Detail section in the bottom of ME21N, it is possible to enter a new schedule line for the same item, without a reference to a requisition, even this workaround has been implemented.

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: Use Calculated Measure and Calculated Dimension in Re...
entry.content:
Hi Edwin

from your example, I would assume that you would like to assign 0 to these measures. Consequently, the syntax should be like this:

scope['Other'] = 0;

You can also refer to other fields in your assignment. In such cases, you would just have to add a "scope" in front of the field name you would like to refer to.

For example:

scope['Other'] = scope.FieldA;

Even If-conditions are possible. Hence, you could define a logic like:

scope['Other'] = scope.FieldA === 'ABC' ? 'Yes' : 'XYZ';

You can also refer to the reports created in the official sample contents. For example in SXD/007 - Corporate Sustainability Management, such calculated measures with simple assignments are used a lot.

The formula you provide in the calcualted measure/dimension will go through all records included in your query. In the example provide above this would mean that, if FieldA has the value 'ABC', you will assign the string 'Yes' to your new dimension called Other. If the value in FieldA is not 'ABC', you will assign 'XYZ' to your new dimension called Other.

I hope this clarifies some of your doubts.

 

Best regards

Benedikt

entry.title: Delete list of delivery adresses
entry.content:
Hi guys,

can you tell me what's the best way to delete multiple address numbers in SAP?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Deleting a vendor contact with vmd_ei_api=>maintain_bapi
entry.content: How do you turn it ON?

entry.title: Re: MIR4 - Add custom fields in vendor data subscreen 651...
entry.content: SHD0 can't add fields. Please don't post an answer for comments, asking or giving additional details. Answer is reserved to propose a solution. Instead, use Replies > Comment (on the question or on a proposed answer).

entry.title: Re: How to change value help dialog table from grid table to responsive table?
entry.content: I've found out that normaly the App changes to responsive table automatically, but in my case not. Strange. I've added the value help via abap cds annotation

entry.title: output purchase order SAP PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content: How are purchase order prints customized in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

entry.title: Re: 175842 Note reversal
entry.content: This method is correct. TCODE "OMJJ" is an IMG setting. You can add tcode 'QA11' to allow Movement Type '349' in Tcode 'OMJJ'.

entry.title: Re: Delete list of delivery adresses
entry.content:
Hi 

I know only to delete delivery address one by one from MEAN Transaction

Search delivery address  select it and go to header you will get address tab and then delete it .

Hope it will help you 

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: MIR4 - Add custom fields in vendor data subscreen 651...

entry.title: Re: How to get the Current Date - 1 . as a Report Header...
entry.content: With out Scripting is this possible to achieve?

entry.title: Re: How to perform a deep update with SAP Gateway OData V4 framework?
entry.content: It is possible with HTTP POST and  the re definition of the CREATE_DEEP_ENTITY method to execute a deep update in oData 2.0. I did not try it with oData 4.0.

entry.title: Re: output purchase order SAP PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content:
Hi 

Please check this blog and SAP note it will help you 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/s-4hana-cloud-output-management-purchase-order/ba-p/12840660

3008187 - Purchase Order Output Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Take logic here and select print here 

Hope it will help you 

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: ABAP dump in background scheduled job
entry.content:
I have the issue, that the sequence is right and the return table is empty. Nevertheless, in INCLUDE LPS_BAPIF01 it raises MESSAGE x030(cnif_pi). Any Ideas? Note 1901102 says on the last bullet point:

  • Commit work is used without a precommit.
    -> If you called the precommit before, please check in BAPI_PS_PRECOMMIT the flag is set in the line:

        call function 'PS_FLAG_SET_GLOBAL_FLAGS'
             exporting
                  i_precommit_ok = 'N'.

    This piece of coding should be called directly before the commit work.

I don't understand that part, as call function 'PS_FLAG_SET_GLOBAL_FLAGS' is already part of BAPI_PS_PRECOMMIT...

entry.title: Re: How to import Test Scripts from the SAP Best Practice content for SAP S/4 HANA Cloud into SAP Cl
entry.content:
Hi Nicolas,
Can you let me know, how I can find the "Best Practice Excel file"?
Thanks.

entry.title: 2H 2024 RCM can't forward as applicant
entry.content:
Since the release, we've noticed in RCM that for any newly created reqs, we cannot forward as applicant, apply to the req (it will post, but if we try to apply we get an error saying it's deleted or no longer available).  If we try to forward an applicant, the req doesn't come up in the search.  However all reqs created prior to the release don't have any issues.

Anyone having the same problems?

entry.title: Re: Mask in CJ20N - "Screen" field
entry.content: In that last screen shot the project definition you entered is one that has a coding mask and you hit Enter?  If so, you should enter a support issue to SAP.

entry.title: Re: How Fetch Highlighted Rows from a SAP Table
entry.content:
Hi,

GuiTableControl organizes the rows in a collection of objects of type GuiTableRow.
See also:
https://help.sap.com/docs/sap_gui_for_windows/b47d018c3b9b45e897faf66a6c0885a8/ce1d9e64355d49568e5def5271aea2db.html
-> Rows

Each row is an object with certain properties. See also:
https://help.sap.com/docs/sap_gui_for_windows/b47d018c3b9b45e897faf66a6c0885a8/5341bac75c8d428eb8460f6c84b13eee.html

And one of the properties is "Selected".

Best regards,
Frank

entry.title: Re: How Fetch Highlighted Rows from a SAP Table
entry.content:
Hi,

GuiTableControl organizes the rows in a collection of objects of type GuiTableRow.
See also:
https://help.sap.com/docs/sap_gui_for_windows/b47d018c3b9b45e897faf66a6c0885a8/ce1d9e64355d49568e5def5271aea2db.html

Each of the rows is an object in this collection and has properties like "Selected". See also: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap_gui_for_windows/b47d018c3b9b45e897faf66a6c0885a8/5341bac75c8d428eb8460f6c84b13eee.html

Best regards,
Frank

entry.title: Re: Connect to azure blob storage using SAS token in SAP...
entry.content:
Hello @harsha_kotni,

You can use ADLS Gen2 as outbound, which has the option to connect via SAS tokens, have implemented this scenario and it worked.

entry.title: Conditions created through migration cockpit with distribution formula empty
entry.content:
Is there a reason why the "Distribution formula" field is not being automatically populated during the migration of Real Estate contracts through the migration cockpit? In customizing, the value "YA" is set as the default, but when contracts are migrated, the field remains empty, causing issues with generating object cash-flow and preventing proper postings to WBS elements, which conflicts with global validation rules.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Conditions created through migration cockpit with dis...
entry.content: Migration cockpit does not support Distribution Formulas for migration object "Real estate contract". The recommended approach is to use Valuation Rules for all objects, including J4 Objects, where Distribution Formulas might otherwise work.

entry.title: Re: AJUSTE SINIEF 13 E 14/24 - Symbolic Return and Symbol...
entry.content:
Dear all,

as I realized that the information about the referenced nota fiscal is coming into VBRK table, I´m just adding an entry in the enhancement, in order to fill up the nota fiscal properly.

entry.title: The Evolving Influence of Procurement in Organisations
entry.content:
A study by Economist Impact, sponsored by SAP, shows procurement teams are breaking down silos to deliver success

In a broad study sponsored by SAP, Economist Impact examines procurement’s growing role, interviewing 2,307 C-suite executives from January to March 2024, spanning multiple countries, regions and industries.

The report finds that external pressures have compelled procurement teams to break down silos and collaborate with other departments to deliver value to organisational goals.

This is not only enhancing cross-functional collaboration but also positioning procurement as a key function in shaping and executing business strategies.

In the past, procurement teams have not been given the same access to strategic decision-making as other departments. They were limited within the scope of the supply chain and forced to make choices based on company policy.

However, this attitude is shifting as procurement executives have more input on long-term company planning.

The report, “Across the Procurement-Verse: Changing Trends in the Procurement Function,” touches on what trends are impacting the growing expectations for procurement.

Gordon Donovan, Global Vice President for Research, Procurement & External Workforce at SAP says: “The research indicates that the trajectory of procurement’s relevance continues.

"The procurement function is increasingly becoming pivotal in shaping and executing business strategies. By breaking down silos and fostering cross-functional collaboration, procurement is not only enhancing operational efficiency but also driving significant value across the organisation.

"However, the opportunity to drive a bigger impact is clear. While the report indicates strong collaboration, with more than 80% of respondents stating that procurement’s insights are essential for their organisational strategies, a gap remains (20%) between value and delivery of these insights. Thus, procurement teams have considerable work to do to ensure that their voice is heard and applied clearly across the business.

“As procurement continues to embrace digital transformation and AI, it is crucial for these teams to align their insights with the needs of various stakeholders. This alignment will further build trust and confidence within the C-suite, ensuring that procurement remains a key player in achieving our long-term organisational goals.”

Interviewees cite lasting effects from the pandemic, inflation rates and other supply chain disruptions as reasons for the increasing reliance on procurement.

Effective collaboration with Chief Operating Officers

With macroeconomic conditions constantly fluctuating, procurement teams are finding themselves reporting to Chief Operating Officers (COOs) rather than Chief Financial Officers (CFOs), reflecting the expanding scope of the procurement team beyond cost reduction to operational risk management.

According to the Economist Impact survey, 75% of respondents agree that procurement collaborates effectively with the rest of the organisation to achieve the company vision, a significant increase from the previous year.

However, with only 18% strongly agreeing, there is still some scope for improvement in procurement collaboration and value creation. “Procurement has often operated in this bubble that was in service of its own goals as opposed to in service of the goals of the wider business,” the report adds.

The survey also suggests that executives in procurement are now increasingly focusing on generating insights that align with the needs of a variety of stakeholders, creating cross-functional collaboration to improve outcomes across the organisation.

AI as a driver for digitalisation

AI is demonstrating it can be used to harness efficiency across the business. Within procurement, AI and generative AI are being used to optimise spend, enhance supplier management, create cost-saving opportunities and allow procurement professionals to focus on value-adding activities.

The Economist Impact data suggests that AI is a significant driver of digital transformation. Accelerating digitalisation is tied as the top procurement priority over the next 12-18 months, with 44% of respondents identifying AI as a crucial technology focus.

Respondents anticipate that AI will bring about significant enhancements in two areas: the automation of procurement processes (48%) and improved guidance and optimisation (45%).

CPOs are witnessing first-hand the direct impacts on business outcomes through increased technology investment. To realise the value of AI, procurement executives must formulate roadmaps for change management, adopt new ways of working and invest in developing new skills for their workforce.

The survey states that 69% of respondents believe a lack of vision and business alignment poses a major change management challenge in procurement transformation.

Inflation’s lasting impact

Cost management for companies remains a high priority on the procurement agenda, especially as high inflation rates become a growing concern for CPOs, with 49% of executives citing monetary uncertainty as their top priority compared to other risks – a 29% jump from 2023. Even while inflation rates have dipped, costs for materials and items remain high.

It is no shock that 71% of the CPO respondents identify macroeconomic risks as one of the primary external factors influencing their organisations’ strategic priorities over the next 12-18 months.

Despite this, it is still difficult to predict when and if the economic turbulence currently being experienced will settle down. Once it does, procurement leaders are expected to divert their attention to risk management, with 40% citing supplier diversification as a priority over the next three to five years.

However, “diversifying from suppliers is not as easy, especially if the supplier is the sole provider of the goods or service, or if they are deeply embedded in the supply chain,” the report says.

Technology can help the team to easily spot alternative sources of materials, ensuring supply chain resilience and minimising the risk of disruptions.

The sustainable buyer

There are also external pressures mounting from internal stakeholders and regulators for companies to act on environmental issues. Procurement teams have placed a much larger emphasis on sustainability.

With 70% of executives saying that legal and regulatory non-compliance is a key external risk, procurement teams have responded accordingly, as 32% of executives list sustainability as a top priority, a 7% increase from 2023. Additionally, the top priority for sustainability among executives is compliance, demonstrating an increased focus on regulatory risks.

This increased attention on environmental, social and governance (ESG) has been successful, as 68% of executives expressed confidence in procurement consistently meeting ESG goals, a 21% increase from last year’s survey.

With long-term sustainability measures in place, procurement teams may serve as a model for regulatory compliance.

Shaping the procurement function of the future

The Economist Impact research shows procurement’s growing value for organisations, with CPOs and the teams they run gaining a seat at the table. But to maintain this position, procurement must address critical areas for improvement, such as spend management and cost control capabilities, where a third of C-suite executives lack confidence in procurement’s abilities.

There are still deeper issues which the survey illustrates, including gaps in procurement’s visibility into stakeholder risks and priorities, limitations in creating accurate spend data, developing clear roadmaps and forging deeper relationships with stakeholders.

Collaboration and improved communication are crucial for building trust and sustaining engagement with procurement’s insights.

Investing in procurement technology, such as SAP Ariba Procurement solutions, can help address priorities and enhance the procurement function. These solutions can span various aspects of procurement and include new integrations and generative AI features to help automate and streamline processes.

Additionally, enhancements to SAP Business Network aim to reduce silos between and within organisations and can result in significant time and cost savings.

entry.title: Re: Premium Hub CoE – DSC SAP TM Archiving TOR Object

entry.title: Issues when standard app 'Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant' (F1957) extension tried via BAS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have the standard app called  'Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant' app ID F1957.

I need to create an adaptation project for this app with field modifications and controller extension. Since this is app is having synchronous view, BAS  suggested to create Extension Project. So, I went with that. But in the next step Create Extension screen is not loading and is blank.

When I checked the console, error is present. Because of which, it was not opening it seems.

Kindly suggest what needs to be done for this issue.

 

Regards,

Bhaskar

is there a report like this in sap

entry.title: Re: supplier invoice workflow
entry.content: Dear Jerry

entry.title: Re: Send Notification Approval Request to Users if the Ve...
entry.content:
Hi 

See these thread I will help you, A little Part I have pasted 

You can define sensitive fields in the vendor master data that if changed/created will require approval.

See trans. SPRO activity: Define Sensitive Fields for Dual Control (Vendors)

You can base a workflow process on this or simply allow approvers to enter trans. FK08 directly.

Use also workflow template 20000001 as an example for removing blocks set on vendor.

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/workflow-for-vendor-master/qaq-p/7045670

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/dual-control-functionality-in-sap/ba-p/13250407

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

AFVGD
AFRUD
CAUFVD
VIQMFE
RIWO00
VIQMUR

 

since they are structures, I can't do a Select, are there tables that I can use that are connected to these structures? Or are there Function Modules?


Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP RAR articel describes the Revenue process. What a...
entry.content:
RAR is able to recognize costs along with revenue. It uses invoices with cost conditions to take costs into consideration. You need to enable company codes for cost recognition. 

Result Analysis is still used to integrate the cost postings with CO.

You can read more about it here:
Integration with Cost Object Controlling | SAP Help Portal


You can integrate RAR with

entry.title: Re: Can I retrieve BKPF values in SAPLFI_ITEMS?
entry.content:
Check the top include of this function group, there's a “GS_BKPF” structure, but depending on where you call, this data may or may not be relevant.

  • This function module (DETERMINE_DUE_DATE) is called from hundreds of programs in the system, so your display will be inconsistent depending on which screens users are viewing.
  • For ALV FI irem display, check FM ITEM_DERIVE_FIELDS which contains RFPOSXEXT
  • What specific calculation is envisaged?

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Launchpad: all tiles open the same app
entry.content:
Hi,

entry.title: Re: Custom Payroll Localization issues with posting to accounting
entry.content:
This is very simple

If you would like see any SAP standard t.codes 
Go to --> SE93 -- > Then Give --> PC00_M*_CIPE --> Click on F4
Then you will see country specific Posting T.codes 
hope this will help you out..!

entry.title: Ariba Risk - Extending Supplier Screens with 3rd Party API Data
entry.content:

I am looking into creating a scorecard screen within supplier risk in Ariba and whether it is possible using the Arba APIs

- the direction needed in terms of skills i.e. front end and middleware BTP, CPI
- any systems pre requisites 
- how long a piece of work like this might take? 
- If there might be any other ways to import external scoring data to ariba risk for a supplier

entry.title: Marina **Call-''Girls** O52*86O*4116 **Call-''Girls** in 𝓓ubai Marina
entry.content:
Dubai Marina After a long wait, the much-anticipated and independent **Call-''Girls** has finally been launched, and people couldn’t be more excited! The Dubai Marina has been the talk of the town, and now that it's available, the excitement has reached new heights. Those who had been eagerly awaiting its release can now get their hands on it and experience its incredible features. If you're one of the many who have been waiting, the good news is that the wait is over! You can now 0589859100 to place your order and be among the first to own this amazing device. The response has been overwhelming, with people thrilled to finally get their marina  **Call-''Girls** and enjoying every moment with it. Don't miss out on the chance to join them—call now and get yours today!

 

After months of anticipation and countless rumors, the wait is finally over! The long-awaited **Call-''Girls** Adorable Dubai marina 0589859100 **Call-''Girls** in (E11) GHADEER AL TAIR has officially launched, and people everywhere are thrilled. For weeks, tech enthusiasts have been eagerly waiting for this moment, and now that the **Call-''Girls** is available, the excitement is through the roof. People are already talking about the stunning design, top-notch features, and cutting-edge technology packed into this Dubai Marina. Those who have been eagerly counting down the days can now take action—it's time to enjoy this incredible piece of technology.

Roland

entry.title: Re: Inactive Pricing Condition
entry.content: Standard behaviour, if PN00 is in low level in your pricing prodecure, the PN00 will deactivate the PR00

entry.title: SAC Data Analyzer - hide shortcut link
entry.content:
Hi SAC Experts,

Is it possible to hide the shortcut link creation in the Data Analyzer, feature released on the 2024 Q3.

Data analyzer – create a shortcut link for quick sharing SAP Road Map. 

Current, with the user role definitions, I already limit the Save and the Create New Insight options. The point here is the end user is consuming the Story in embed mode and would like to keep it away from the SAC tenant.

My SAC version is:

2024.15.15 (Client)
2024.15.13 (Server)

Data Analyzer - shortcut link

SAP Analytics Cloud 

Thank you,

Rui

entry.title: Hide the option of creating form from one employee
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am facing a particular issue:

We have one employee group which has been given one role, for all the users with the role of employee. We have four 360 templates in the system which are:

 

  1. 1A - anonymized
  2. 1B - non - anonymized
  3. 2A - anonymized
  4. 2B - non - anonymized


We want to create four groups of employees and we want to give them all the permission to create forms in the 'My Performance' page. The condition is, employee of one group should not be able to create form for the other templates, as this can lead to increase in redundant number of tickets. I am attaching a screenshot where in the 'Create Form' page, all the form templates are available. I want to customize this for different groups of employees.

Also, this needs to be done without creating 4 different permission roles and groups.

Let me know how one can do this.
